p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Recovering. The reduction in visible wilting compared to the earliest image suggests the soil amendment is aiding moisture retention.

p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Stable. The plant is holding its current state despite the challenging VPD environment.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t5: Significant intervention occurred; white amendments added to p2/p4 soil.
temporal_deltas.t5_to_current: The biome has entered a maintenance phase; no further drastic changes in plant posture or soil texture.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of 'Managed Stability'. The user's intervention in T-5 has successfully mitigated the acute stress observed in the earliest image.
vision_report.anomalies: None. The white material on the soil is identified as a successful user-applied amendment.
vision_report.narrative_description: The garden has transitioned from a state of acute stress to a stable, managed environment. The addition of soil amendments in the p2/p4 pot has stabilized the moisture profile. All plants are currently showing signs of acclimation to the indoor VPD conditions.
